lastLearnRun: 2026-05-11 email brand audit r2 cleanup: source-grep-contract-tests, silent-skip-in-legal-compliance-checks, magic-zero-for-file-level-findings, cargo-cult-defensive-no-op, bolting-new-domain-onto-existing-scanroots, config-pinning-tests-disguised-as-behavior-tests

antiPatterns.description: AnimatedSection or any Framer Motion entrance without useReducedMotion()
antiPatterns.avoid: motion.div with initial/whileInView and no reduceMotion check; globals.css only affects CSS, not JS-driven motion
antiPatterns.instead: useReducedMotion(); when true render plain div (or motion with duration: 0); when false use motion props

antiPatterns.description: Section with hardcoded paragraphs when same content exists in data
antiPatterns.avoid:
FounderSection with three fixed

while founder.bio holds the text
antiPatterns.instead: Drive from data: founder.bio.split(/(?<=.)\s+/).filter(Boolean).map(...) or bioParagraphs in JSON

antiPatterns.description: Entire page is client when only some parts need interactivity
antiPatterns.avoid: 'use client' on page so schema and all sections run on client
antiPatterns.instead: Server Component page: render JSON-LD on server; single client wrapper (e.g. PageClient) for nav, hero, StickyCTA

antiPatterns.description: List key by name when slug or order is available
antiPatterns.avoid: key={coach.name} when coach.slug or coach.order exist; names can duplicate or slug null
antiPatterns.instead: key={coach.slug ?? `order-${coach.order}`} for stable unique keys

antiPatterns.description: JSON-LD builder using optional fields without guard or fallback
antiPatterns.avoid: coach.image.startsWith('http') without check; description: coach.schemaDescription with no fallback; hardcoded SITE_URL
antiPatterns.instead: Guard image; description: coach.schemaDescription ?? coach.bio ?? ''; SITE_URL from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL with fallback

antiPatterns.description: Anchor targets (#hash) without scroll-margin when sticky nav is present
antiPatterns.avoid: Section id="team" with no scroll-mt; scroll lands with heading under sticky nav
antiPatterns.instead: Add scroll-mt-28 (or match nav height) to section roots that are anchor targets

antiPatterns.description: Focus ring too weak on dark background (e.g. ring-white/30)
antiPatterns.avoid: focus:ring-white/30 on DarkSection CTAs; may fail focus-indicator contrast
antiPatterns.instead: focus:ring-white or focus:ring-white/60; or rely on global focus-visible

antiPatterns.description: New components using text-lbta-slate instead of brand tokens
antiPatterns.avoid: text-lbta-slate in new CoachCard, FounderSection; .cursorrules say new code use brand-*
antiPatterns.instead: text-brand-pacific-dusk/70 (or appropriate opacity) for secondary text

antiPatterns.description: Hardcoding prices or tier amounts in components
antiPatterns.avoid: PricingComparison tiers with $50, $546 in TSX; LeaguesSection $48/year in copy
antiPatterns.instead: Source from /data/*.json or lib/programs-data; component only renders

antiPatterns.description: Same list of programs/options defined in multiple places
antiPatterns.avoid: TrialBookingModal programs array and app/book/page.tsx programs constant
antiPatterns.instead: Single source (data file or lib helper); both modal and page import

antiPatterns.description: Returning 200 with success: false on webhook processing failure
antiPatterns.avoid: catch block returns NextResponse.json({ success: false }) with no status
antiPatterns.instead: NextResponse.json(..., { status: 500 }) for server/processing errors

antiPatterns.description: Closing a modal without restoring focus to the trigger
antiPatterns.avoid: handleClose only calls onClose(); user tab lands nowhere predictable
antiPatterns.instead: Save document.activeElement (or trigger ref) on open; focus() in close

antiPatterns.description: Icon-only button with no accessible name
antiPatterns.avoid: <button><SendIcon /></button>
antiPatterns.instead: aria-label="Send message" or visible text

antiPatterns.description: UI component that POSTs to an API route that does not exist
antiPatterns.avoid: ChatWidget POST /api/chat with no app/api/chat/route.ts
antiPatterns.instead: Implement the route with validation and rate limit, or disable the UI

antiPatterns.description: Making webhook verification skip when secret env is unset
antiPatterns.avoid: if (!secret) return null; // skip verification
antiPatterns.instead: In production, require secret and reject request when unset

antiPatterns.description: Keeping state or refs that are set but never read
antiPatterns.avoid: isMobile, setIsMobile + resize effect; containerRef with no .current read
antiPatterns.instead: Remove before merge (YAGNI)

antiPatterns.description: API route reimplements JSON parse with request.json() try/catch when parseJsonBody exists
antiPatterns.avoid: try { body = await request.json() } catch { return 400 } in one route while others use parseJsonBody
antiPatterns.instead: Use parseJsonBody(request) from lib/validations for every JSON route; return parsed.response when !parsed.ok

antiPatterns.description: Modal that uses setTimeout/setInterval does not clear it on unmount
antiPatterns.avoid: setTimeout id stored but cleanup never runs clearTimeout; setState may run after unmount
antiPatterns.instead: Store id in ref; useEffect cleanup: return () => { if (ref.current) clearTimeout(ref.current); }

antiPatterns.description: Modal that auto-closes (e.g. success timeout) does not restore focus
antiPatterns.avoid: Only manual close restores focus; timeout callback only setVisible(false)
antiPatterns.instead: In timeout callback: previousActiveRef.current?.focus(); then setVisible(false)

antiPatterns.description: Using Sunset Cliff (orange) as primary CTA fill on page heroes
antiPatterns.avoid: bg-brand-sunset-cliff for main hero or primary section CTA
antiPatterns.instead: Black/white or .btn-primary per .cursorrules; Sunset Cliff for hover/accent only

antiPatterns.description: Footer or dark-section text at low opacity failing WCAG 7:1
antiPatterns.avoid: text-white/40 or text-white/25 on bg-brand-deep-water for body or tagline
antiPatterns.instead: Use text-white/50 or higher (or verify 7:1 with WebAIM); secondary text at least /50

antiPatterns.description: Primary hero button with only text color on dark background
antiPatterns.avoid: text-black or text-white with no background on dark gradient (e.g. from-black/75)
antiPatterns.instead: Use solid background (bg-white text-black or bg-black text-white) so contrast meets WCAG

antiPatterns.description: Using payload ID in webhook URLs or request bodies without validating
antiPatterns.avoid: contactId = data.contact?.id; then `${baseUrl}/contacts/${contactId}` without type/length check
antiPatterns.instead: Normalize to positive integer; return 400 if invalid; use only the validated number in URLs and bodies

antiPatterns.description: Comparing webhook secret with == or === (timing side-channel)
antiPatterns.avoid: if (headerSecret === AC_WEBHOOK_SECRET) return null
antiPatterns.instead: Buffer.from both; ensure same length; crypto.timingSafeEqual(expected, provided); reject on mismatch or length diff

antiPatterns.description: Parallax or scroll-driven animation without checking prefers-reduced-motion
antiPatterns.avoid: useEffect scroll listener that updates transform/parallax state on every scroll with no reduceMotion check
antiPatterns.instead: Gate with matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'); when true disable parallax or use static fallback (same as HomeHero)

antiPatterns.description: Blockquote with solid left border instead of Brand Guide pull-quote style
antiPatterns.avoid: border-l-2 border-brand-pacific-dusk/30 or similar on blockquote
antiPatterns.instead: Use <PullQuote> or class section-quote for gradient left edge (Victoria Cove → Sunset Cliff); single source for quote styling

antiPatterns.description: Decorative SVG (arrow, icon) inside link or button without aria-hidden
antiPatterns.avoid: <Link><svg>...</svg> Text</Link> with no aria-hidden on svg
antiPatterns.instead: Add aria-hidden="true" to the svg so screen readers don't announce the icon

antiPatterns.description: Using Tailwind color class not defined in tailwind.config (e.g. lbta-tan)
antiPatterns.avoid: bg-lbta-tan or text-lbta-tan when lbta.tan is not in config
antiPatterns.instead: Use only defined tokens: lbta-beige or brand-sandstone for warm backgrounds; grep config before adding custom color classes

antiPatterns.description: Form error or required text using raw Tailwind red (text-red-400, text-red-500, text-red-800)
antiPatterns.avoid: text-red-400, text-red-800 for error message; text-red-500 for required asterisk; bg-red-50 border-red-200 for error box
antiPatterns.instead: Use text-lbta-red for error and required; error callout use bg-lbta-red/5 border-lbta-red/20; dismiss button text-lbta-red hover:opacity-80

antiPatterns.description: HorizonDivider with optional as prop (e.g. as="hr" or as="div")
antiPatterns.avoid: Passing as prop to HorizonDivider; component was simplified to always render <hr>
antiPatterns.instead: Use HorizonDivider with variant and className only; no as prop (YAGNI)

antiPatterns.description: Hero with short min-height and bottom-aligned content clips headline at top
antiPatterns.avoid: min-h-[70vh] (or similar) with absolute bottom-0 content and overflow-hidden; on short viewports content block is taller than section and top gets clipped
antiPatterns.instead: Use min-h-screen so section is always full viewport height; full headline (e.g. 'Tennis, as it should be taught') stays visible

antiPatterns.description: Logging full response body when a third-party API returns an error
antiPatterns.avoid: console.error('[GHL] Create failed:', res.status, await res.text()); full body can contain PII or internal IDs
antiPatterns.instead: Truncate: const safeMsg = text.length > 100 ? text.slice(0, 100) + '…' : text; log res.status and safeMsg only

antiPatterns.description: Script that calls external API (e.g. Vercel) puts full response body in thrown Error or log
antiPatterns.avoid: if (!res.ok) return res.text().then(t => Promise.reject(new Error(res.status + ': ' + t))); then catch logs err.message (which contains body)
antiPatterns.instead: Reject with status only: Promise.reject(new Error('Vercel API error: ' + res.status)); never include response body in error or log

antiPatterns.description: Form handler returns 500 when optional integration (GHL, storeLead) or rate limit fails after validation passed
antiPatterns.avoid: await sendToGHL(...) or void sendToGHL(...) without .catch(); rateLimit() unguarded so KV failure throws; parseJsonBody throws on non-JSON body
antiPatterns.instead: Wrap rateLimit in try/catch and allow on failure; parseJsonBody returns { ok: false, status: 400 } on any parse error; void integration().catch(e => console.error(...)); return 200 when validation passes

antiPatterns.description: Using text-gray-* in sections that should use brand tokens (e.g. PartnershipSection)
antiPatterns.avoid: text-gray-500, text-gray-600, group-hover:text-gray-700 in components that are part of the main brand experience
antiPatterns.instead: Use text-brand-pacific-dusk/60, text-brand-pacific-dusk/80, group-hover:text-brand-pacific-dusk (and border-black/6 for borders) per design system

antiPatterns.description: API route calls rateLimit() without try/catch so KV failure throws and returns 500
antiPatterns.avoid: const rateLimitResult = await rateLimit(...); if (!rateLimitResult.allowed) return 429; with no try/catch around rateLimit
antiPatterns.instead: Wrap rateLimit() in try/catch; on catch use fallback { allowed: true, remaining: maxRequests, resetTime: Date.now() + interval }; apply in all form routes (book, newsletter, register, etc.)

antiPatterns.description: Client component imports season data JSON when lib provides a getter
antiPatterns.avoid: import winter2026Data from '@/data/winter2026.json' in 'use client' component; using winter2026Data.programs
antiPatterns.instead: Import from @/lib/programs-data and use getWinter2026Programs() (or equivalent); single entry point; avoids duplicating JSON in client bundle

antiPatterns.description: Price display uses only session prices (1x/2x/3x) when program also has monthly
antiPatterns.avoid: seasonPrices = [oneX, twoX, threeX]; return min(seasonPrices) without checking if monthly is primary or lower (e.g. Little Stars shows From $200/season)
antiPatterns.instead: If monthly != null and (no session prices OR monthly <= min(sessionPrices)), return { amount: monthly, label: '/mo' }; else use session logic

antiPatterns.description: Lib that re-exports or transforms data has exports that are never used
antiPatterns.avoid: getSpringPrograms() and getSummerPrograms() in programs-data when only *ForDisplay variants are used
antiPatterns.instead: Remove unused exports; grep for usages; keep single way to get 'programs for UI' per season

antiPatterns.description: Same program has different display name or ages in different season JSON files
antiPatterns.avoid: Winter: 'Adult Beginner 1', ages 'True Beginner'; Spring-Summer: 'Adult Beginner 1 — True Beginner', 'NTRP 1.0-2.0'
antiPatterns.instead: Use identical program name and ages string across winter, spring-summer, fall so UI and data consumers see consistent copy

antiPatterns.description: Webhook endpoint without rate limiting
antiPatterns.avoid: POST handler for external webhooks (e.g. ActiveCampaign) with no rateLimit() call
antiPatterns.instead: rateLimit('webhook:'+ip, RATE_LIMITS.webhook) at start with try/catch; allow on failure. Return 401 in production when secret unset; no PII in prod logs

antiPatterns.description: Two implementations of same component (e.g. root vs ui/)
antiPatterns.avoid: components/AnimatedSection.tsx and components/ui/AnimatedSection.tsx both defining the component
antiPatterns.instead: One canonical (e.g. ui/); legacy path re-exports so existing imports resolve to same implementation

antiPatterns.description: Registration modal pricing duplicated in lib/form-config.ts instead of from /data
antiPatterns.avoid: prePopulateData.pricing in FORM_CONFIGS with literal strings ($260, $546, $495/week, etc.) that duplicate data/winter2026.json, year2026.json
antiPatterns.instead: Derive modal pricing from programs-data, camps-data, or a single shared config in /data; or document sync requirement and run data-integrity validation periodically

antiPatterns.description: Generic emoji (🎾 📅 💰 📞) in buttons or links
antiPatterns.avoid: Chatbot quick-reply buttons or any UI using emoji as primary icon; .cursorrules forbid generic emoji icons
antiPatterns.instead: Use text labels only or non-generic icons (e.g. Lucide); keeps brand and a11y consistent

antiPatterns.description: Using 'as unknown as T' at server→client data boundary when type could come from schema
antiPatterns.avoid: parseYear2026Sections(data) as unknown as Year2026Sections with Year2026Sections defined only in client
antiPatterns.instead: Export type from lib (e.g. schedule-schemas.ts: export type Year2026Sections = Year2026SectionsValidated); client imports that type; no cast

antiPatterns.description: Putting turbopack config under experimental in Next.js 16
antiPatterns.avoid: experimental: { turbo: { root: __dirname } } — Next 16 reports 'Unrecognized key turbo at experimental'
antiPatterns.instead: Top-level turbopack: { root: __dirname } in next.config.js (Next 15.3+ / 16)

antiPatterns.description: Schedule slot note uses abbreviation or different wording than other slot-level venue text in the same file
antiPatterns.avoid: slot note: 'LBHS' while other programs use 'LB High School' in slot location/note
antiPatterns.instead: Use same venue wording everywhere in that file (e.g. 'LB High School' not 'LBHS') so Pattern Recognizer and UI stay consistent

antiPatterns.description: lib/form-config.ts prePopulateData.location for a program does not match the program location in season JSON
antiPatterns.avoid: Youth Development location: 'Alta Laguna Park' in form-config while data/winter2026.json (and other seasons) use 'Laguna Beach High School'
antiPatterns.instead: Keep form-config location aligned with canonical program location from data/*.json; when editing season data, check and update form-config for that program

antiPatterns.description: Program description in season JSON includes date-specific text that will age (e.g. starting April)
antiPatterns.avoid: description: '... starting April. Same pricing ...' in spring-summer-2026.json
antiPatterns.instead: Use location or season context only; avoid calendar dates unless intentionally time-bound and documented for update each season

antiPatterns.description: Middleware protects a route (e.g. /coach-hub) with env secret but returns next() when secret unset
antiPatterns.avoid: if (!secret) return NextResponse.next(); so hub is reachable without auth
antiPatterns.instead: When path is protected and secret missing, redirect to /coach-hub/login or return 503; never allow access when unconfigured

antiPatterns.description: Modal/overlay without focus trap and Escape-to-close
antiPatterns.avoid: Dialog with no onKeyDown(Escape); focus can leave; keyboard users cannot close
antiPatterns.instead: Add onKeyDown(Escape → onClose) and focus trap (e.g. focus first focusable on open)

antiPatterns.description: Clear-cookie header does not set Secure when session cookie was set with Secure in production
antiPatterns.avoid: buildCoachHubClearCookie() returns Max-Age=0 without Secure; some browsers may not clear over HTTPS
antiPatterns.instead: Include Secure in clear-cookie string when NODE_ENV === 'production', matching set-cookie

antiPatterns.description: Using new Function() to parse input in extraction scripts
antiPatterns.avoid: new Function('return (' + s + ')')(); if s ever comes from untrusted source, code injection
antiPatterns.instead: Use safe parser (e.g. bracket-counting) or document that script runs only against trusted HTML; avoid repurposing for untrusted input

antiPatterns.description: CTA or submit button with min-width larger than smallest viewport causing horizontal scroll
antiPatterns.avoid: min-w-[300px] on a button inside a padded container at 320px viewport (e.g. apply-scholarship submit)
antiPatterns.instead: Use w-full with optional max-w-[300px], or min-w-0 in a flex container so button does not force overflow; audit at 320px

antiPatterns.description: Form inputs with font-size below 16px on mobile (triggers iOS zoom on focus)
antiPatterns.avoid: text-[15px] or text-[14px] on input, select, textarea for user-facing forms (Contact, registration modals)
antiPatterns.instead: Use text-base or text-[16px] for inputs so iOS does not zoom on focus; labels and secondary text may stay smaller

antiPatterns.description: Auth API that returns 400/401 but no way to verify at runtime when secret is set (e.g. dev has secret unset)
antiPatterns.avoid: Validation summary warns 'verify auth 400/401 in staging'; no script or doc to run the check
antiPatterns.instead: Add a small script (e.g. scripts/verify-coach-hub-auth.mjs) that fetch POST {} and POST { password: 'wrong' } and assert 400/401; run with dev server started with secret set; document in validation summary or README

antiPatterns.description: List of expandable cards (e.g. ProgramCard) each with a fixed/sticky bar when expanded, but no single-expand
antiPatterns.avoid: Each card manages its own isExpanded state; multiple cards can be expanded → multiple fixed bottom bars stack; wrong program may open on Register
antiPatterns.instead: Lift expanded id to parent: expandedProgramId; pass isExpanded and onToggle to each card so only one can be expanded; single sticky bar in DOM. See pattern single-expand-cards-in-list.

antiPatterns.description: Form/booking API returns raw Zod validation.error in 400 response
antiPatterns.avoid: NextResponse.json({ success: false, error: validation.error }, { status: 400 }) — exposes field names and internal messages to client
antiPatterns.instead: Log validation.error server-side; return generic message (e.g. 'Invalid request. Please check your input.') or sanitized field errors only

antiPatterns.description: Thank-you or confirmation page with tel, mailto, Return, or app store links but no visible focus ring
antiPatterns.avoid: Links without focus:ring-* so keyboard users have no visible focus indicator
antiPatterns.instead: Add focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-victoria-cove focus:ring-offset-2 rounded-sm (or ring-white on dark) to every focusable link

antiPatterns.description: Thank-you or confirmation page step/card styling with raw blue (blue-600, blue-100)
antiPatterns.avoid: text-blue-600, bg-blue-100 in step numbers or cards; bypasses brand palette
antiPatterns.instead: Use brand tokens (e.g. text-brand-victoria-cove, bg-brand-victoria-cove/10) for steps and accents

antiPatterns.description: Pull quote using both .section-quote and an extra border (e.g. border-l-2 border-brand-victoria-cove/40)
antiPatterns.avoid: blockquote with section-quote and border-l-2 border-brand-victoria-cove/40; Brand Guide defines section-quote with gradient left edge
antiPatterns.instead: Use section-quote only; do not add border-l or border-brand-* on the same element

antiPatterns.description: next.config images.localPatterns omits a prefix used by next/image (Next 15+)
antiPatterns.avoid: Only { pathname: '/images/**' } while Header/Footer/trials use /logos/*.png or /photos/*.webp → production _next/image returns 400 INVALID_IMAGE_OPTIMIZE_REQUEST; browser shows broken image + alt text
antiPatterns.instead: Grep for Image src="/…"; add each top-level folder to localPatterns (/logos/**, /photos/**, etc.)

antiPatterns.description: Fixed header uses semi-transparent light background over dark full-viewport hero
antiPatterns.avoid: bg-brand-morning-light/90 + backdrop-blur over HomeHero video; composited bar reads dark while nav stays text-brand-pacific-dusk
antiPatterns.instead: Opaque bg-brand-morning-light; optional subtle border-b; or dedicated inverse header theme with light text

antiPatterns.description: ChatWidget close button without min-h-[48px] and focus-visible:ring
antiPatterns.avoid: 32px × 32px close button with inline styles, no focus ring, opacity transitions only
antiPatterns.instead: min-h-[48px] min-w-[48px] with Tailwind classes, focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-sunset-cliff focus-visible:ring-offset-2

antiPatterns.description: useCallback includes messages array in dependency array causing unnecessary re-renders
antiPatterns.avoid: const sendMessage = useCallback(async () => { ... }, [input, isLoading, messages])
antiPatterns.instead: Use functional setState (prev => [...prev, ...]) and build history array from updated state inside callback; remove messages from deps

antiPatterns.description: Using legacy lbta-* tokens (lbta-primary, lbta-secondary, lbta-bone) instead of brand-* tokens
antiPatterns.avoid: text-lbta-primary, text-lbta-secondary, bg-lbta-bone in new components or pages
antiPatterns.instead: Use brand-pacific-dusk, brand-pacific-dusk/70, brand-sandstone, brand-morning-light per .cursorrules Part 7

antiPatterns.description: Using focus:ring instead of focus-visible:ring on interactive elements
antiPatterns.avoid: focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-victoria-cove (applies on mouse click)
antiPatterns.instead: focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-victoria-cove focus-visible:ring-offset-2 (only on keyboard focus)

antiPatterns.description: Player photos spread across multiple directories without canonical path
antiPatterns.avoid: Same player photo duplicated in /images/success-stories/, /images/results/, /images/homepage/ with different filenames or crops
antiPatterns.instead: One canonical file per player photo (e.g. /images/success-stories/{slug}.webp); all surfaces reference the same path; plan migration to /images/players/{slug}/ for multi-photo support

antiPatterns.description: Using object-cover on photos with people without specifying objectPosition
antiPatterns.avoid: object-cover with default center position on player/coach photos; heads get cropped in wide containers
antiPatterns.instead: Always set objectPosition (e.g. 50% 10% for top-weighted player shots, 50% 44% for mid-body shots); store in data JSON per image for tuning

antiPatterns.description: New feature or page referencing /legacy-working-assets/ paths
antiPatterns.avoid: Adding src="/legacy-working-assets/..." in new components; deepens dependency on sunset directory
antiPatterns.instead: Copy/migrate asset to /images/ (WebP preferred); update reference; document in docs/photo-map-routing.md. Legacy has 30+ remaining references to migrate.

antiPatterns.description: form-config prePopulateData.duration hardcoded when season dates exist in JSON
antiPatterns.avoid: duration: '8 Saturdays (Apr 11 - May 30)' in form-config while leagues-2026.json has different seasonLabel
antiPatterns.instead: Derive via helper (getUtrCircuitFormDuration) that reads from JSON; add test asserting duration matches season

antiPatterns.description: Using data.program for UTR division tag lookup when data.division exists
antiPatterns.avoid: getUtrDivisionTag(data.program) when payload has division: 'Color Ball' and program: 'UTR Match Play Series — Season 1'
antiPatterns.instead: Use data.division || data.program for getUtrDivisionTag; register-year receives division from modal

antiPatterns.description: Slug/lookup key from label with en-dash (U+2013) fails ASCII-hyphen map
antiPatterns.avoid: Division 'UTR 2.0–4.0' → toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-') yields 'utr-2.0–4.0' which does not match 'utr-2.0-4.0'
antiPatterns.instead: Normalize unicode dashes: .replace(/[\u2013\u2014\u2212]/g, '-') before slug lookup

antiPatterns.description: Program landing using mailto as primary CTA when LuxuryYearModal and API route exist
antiPatterns.avoid: Primary button opens mailto; modal and register-year API unused; no AC tags, no CRM flow
antiPatterns.instead: Wire LuxuryYearModal as primary CTA; keep mailto as secondary for edge questions

antiPatterns.description: Carousel slide imageAlt names a specific person while the image URL shows a different subject or a shared asset meant for another slide
antiPatterns.avoid: Ryan slide with imageAlt 'Ryan Seggerman on a blue hard court' while src is olov_hero_yiuwtr.jpg used also for Olov
antiPatterns.instead: Use one canonical photo per person, or write imageAlt to describe visible content without mis-attributing identity; fix when dedicated asset is uploaded

antiPatterns.description: Interactive planner or quiz outputs dollar amounts from client-side formulas not backed by /data/*.json
antiPatterns.avoid: pathwayPlanner monthlyMin/monthlyMax hardcoded; displayed as if authoritative tuition
antiPatterns.instead: Link to /schedules for rates; optionally show qualitative commitment only; if ranges ever come from data, import from a single JSON module shared with schedules

antiPatterns.description: Page body says unavailable or not offered but meta description invites users to view programs generically
antiPatterns.avoid: match-play og:description implying full program list when hero says not offered
antiPatterns.instead: Rewrite metadata to match expectation; add CTAs to the closest live substitutes (e.g. UTR page, schedules)

antiPatterns.description: Global layout preloads an image that only the homepage needs for LCP
antiPatterns.avoid: <link rel=preload as=image href=/images/hero/hero-poster.webp> in root layout for every route
antiPatterns.instead: Let homepage HomeHero own the fetch via next/image priority; remove duplicate preload from layout

antiPatterns.description: Same poster URL requested via layout preload, video poster attribute, and next/image without deduping
antiPatterns.avoid: preload + poster="..." on video + priority Image all pointing at identical hero-poster.webp
antiPatterns.instead: Single next/image priority layer + video without poster; or one preload OR image priority, not both

antiPatterns.description: Roster batch save uses separate upsert and insert operations for one logical write
antiPatterns.avoid: rowsWithId upsert + rowsWithoutId insert in separate API calls, allowing partial persistence on failure
antiPatterns.instead: Use single transactional RPC for entire batch payload (upsert_players_batch)

antiPatterns.description: Roster endpoint allows duplicate names after normalization
antiPatterns.avoid: Accepting 'Alice Test' and ' alice   test ' as separate players
antiPatterns.instead: Normalize and reject duplicates in payload and existing roster with conflict response

antiPatterns.description: Color Ball week badge payload trusts raw badge arrays with duplicates
antiPatterns.avoid: Passing duplicate badge IDs through to write path, relying on conflict behavior
antiPatterns.instead: Dedupe badges in API layer and DISTINCT in SQL write function

antiPatterns.description: Repeating supabase db push attempts while migration history mismatch is unresolved
antiPatterns.avoid: Retrying db push after 'Remote migration versions not found' without repair
antiPatterns.instead: Run targeted migration repair then db push using latest CLI

antiPatterns.description: Setting alternates.canonical in root layout.tsx cascades to every child page that doesn't override — collapses the site into one URL in Google's index
antiPatterns.avoid: export const metadata = { alternates: { canonical: '/' } } in app/layout.tsx when child pages don't set their own alternates
antiPatterns.instead: Leave alternates out of root layout; set self-referencing alternates.canonical per page (or per feature-section layout). Use root layout ONLY for metadataBase.

antiPatterns.description: When both app/robots.txt and public/robots.txt exist, Next.js App Router serves app/robots.txt — edits to public/robots.txt are silently ignored by the live site
antiPatterns.avoid: Editing public/robots.txt without checking whether app/robots.txt (or app/robots.ts) exists
antiPatterns.instead: Run: ls app/robots.* public/robots.* — if app/robots.* exists, edit that file and delete public/robots.txt to eliminate shadow. One source of truth.

antiPatterns.description: Searching only for middleware.ts when verifying route auth — misses Next.js 16 proxy.ts (the renamed routing middleware file)
antiPatterns.avoid: Glob middleware.* at repo root and conclude 'no auth middleware' when proxy.ts exists
antiPatterns.instead: Grep for both middleware.ts AND proxy.ts at repo root. Next.js 16 renamed the file but semantics are identical (NextRequest → NextResponse with matcher config).

antiPatterns.description: Prioritizing P0 SEO fixes based on audit claims without reading the actual files — wastes engineering time on non-issues and hides real bugs the audit missed
antiPatterns.avoid: Reading an external SEO audit and immediately queuing P0 commits from the report
antiPatterns.instead: Per flagged file: read the file, confirm the claim, THEN prioritize. Every claim has three possible outcomes (confirmed / wrong / partially correct with different fix). Separately: find what the audit missed by checking revenue-critical paths.

antiPatterns.description: Confusing an input `placeholder='...'` attribute (UX hint shown in empty fields) with a displayed phone number — not a broken conversion path even when the placeholder value looks fake
antiPatterns.avoid: Framing placeholder='(949) 123-4567' as 'prospects literally can't reach you' — it's UX hint text that disappears when the user types
antiPatterns.instead: Distinguish placeholder/hint text from actual contact info rendered in copy. Fix placeholders for trust/polish; framing should match the real impact.

antiPatterns.description: Setting `title: 'Something | Brand'` on a child page's metadata when the root layout declares `metadata.title.template: '%s | Brand'` — template appends brand AGAIN, producing '... | Brand | Brand' in the live <title> tag
antiPatterns.avoid: In a child page: `export const metadata = { title: 'FAQ | Tennis Training Questions | LBTA' }` when root layout has `title.template: '%s | Laguna Beach Tennis Academy'` — final title becomes 'FAQ | Tennis Training Questions | LBTA | Laguna Beach Tennis Academy' (83 chars, brand duplicated)
antiPatterns.instead: Segment-only titles: `title: 'Tennis Training FAQ'` — template resolves to 'Tennis Training FAQ | Laguna Beach Tennis Academy' (49 chars). OR bypass template with `title: { absolute: 'Full Custom Title' }` when you need total control.

antiPatterns.description: Replacing composite / unpermissioned testimonials with a visible 'Read reviews on Google' link + star count + 'N Google reviews' callout as the substitute — reads as consumer-grade review-fishing, breaks boutique hospitality aesthetic
antiPatterns.avoid: `<Star>` row + `{rating} from {reviewCount}+ Google reviews` + `Read reviews on Google →` CTA inside any visible section labeled 'What Players Say' or equivalent
antiPatterns.instead: Let earned editorial (named player journeys with photography + outcomes) carry trust. If no editorial exists, omit the block entirely — restraint. Keep AggregateRating in JSON-LD only for SERP stars. Heritage-flavored facts ('Founded 2020', 'Official City Partner') work better than ratings as trust stats.

antiPatterns.description: Validating title/description character-length constraints against the source string in a page's metadata export, when the rendered output may be longer due to template interpolation or additional markup
antiPatterns.avoid: Counting `title: 'FAQ | ...' = 38 chars` and declaring it under 60 — ignores the root layout's `title.template: '%s | Brand'` that adds 31 chars at render time
antiPatterns.instead: Measure the RENDERED <title> tag content in the static build output (`.next/server/app/*.html`) or via live curl. Same rule for <meta name="description"> content and any other length-gated field.

antiPatterns.description: Codifying a number/threshold from a single SMS quote (inbound or outbound) as policy in the bot KB without founder confirmation. SMS reflects what was said in the moment, not what's policy.
antiPatterns.avoid: Reading 'we don't have enough players for tonight — we need at least 2' in an outbound SMS and writing 'LiveBall needs at least 2 players to run' as a Q&A in the bot KB.
antiPatterns.instead: Either ask founder via AskQuestion (live), flag as an open reconciliation item in the KB's Appendix C until verified, or quote vaguely without a number ('if we don't have enough players to run the format'). Move from Open → Resolved with timestamp once founder confirms. This loop's correct value was 5, not 2.

antiPatterns.description: Setting `ages: 'NTRP 2.5+'` while `description` says 'Intermediate (3.0–4.0) and Advanced (4.0+)' and `schedule[*].note` says 'Intermediate (3.0–4.0)' — three different truths in the same JSON record. The bot will pick whichever one its retrieval surfaces first.
antiPatterns.avoid: Updating only one of (ages, description, schedule.note) when founder corrects a level band — leaving stale truth in the other two fields.
antiPatterns.instead: When founder corrects an NTRP/UTR band on a program, update ALL THREE fields in the same record (`ages`, `description`, every `schedule[*].note`) and run the acceptance script. data/{winter,spring-summer,fall}2026.json LiveBall block was the example: ages='NTRP 3.0+', description='Intermediate (3.0–4.0) and Advanced (4.0+)', every non-Sunday-10:30 row has note='Intermediate (3.0–4.0)'.

antiPatterns.description: A bot/AI knowledge base Q&A that asserts a price/time/policy without a `**Sources:**` line pointing to a JSON path or named source. Future audits cannot verify; future founder corrections cannot grep the dependent answers.
antiPatterns.avoid: Q&A like 'Adult Intermediate is $580 for the season' without any source pointer.
antiPatterns.instead: Every Q&A in `gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/23-*.md` ends with `**Sources:** data/spring-summer-2026.json — id: adult-intermediate` and either `**Escalate:** (949) 534-0457` or `**Self-serve:** /schedules`. Verified by grep at acceptance time. Acceptance script can grep `^### Q\d+\.` and assert each Q has `**Sources:**` within N lines below.

antiPatterns.description: Form submit handler that only relies on disabled={isSubmitting} to prevent double submission — useState updates batch async, so rapid clicks fire onSubmit twice before the disabled prop renders
antiPatterns.avoid: const handleSubmit = async (e) => { e.preventDefault(); setIsSubmitting(true); /* network call */ } with no synchronous guard; production data shows 14 duplicate (email,source) pairs within 25-95s windows
antiPatterns.instead: Add synchronous useRef guard: const submittingRef = useRef(false); in handler: if (submittingRef.current) return; submittingRef.current = true; setIsSubmitting(true); release in finally. Pair with server-side dedup window in the storeLead-equivalent function

antiPatterns.description: Claiming 'leads are being saved' or 'we have N leads' based only on the storage target the codebase writes to — ignores parallel paths (Meta lead ads, Zapier, n8n, manual imports) that write directly to a CRM or Notion
antiPatterns.avoid: Querying Supabase leads table, finding 86 rows, telling founder that's the complete lead set. Real total (LBTA): 251 in Notion DB, 165 of which never touched Supabase because they came from Meta lead-ad forms via Zapier
antiPatterns.instead: Enumerate every storage target before answering: Supabase, Notion DB, ActiveCampaign, GoHighLevel, any Zapier/n8n integrations, manual CSV imports. Query each. Report the union and explain the gaps. Recommend a unified mirror pattern (Notion → Supabase cron) when gaps are persistent

antiPatterns.description: Suggesting integration edits to an API route based on its filename or URL convention without reading the route file first — risks reanimating deprecated code
antiPatterns.avoid: Suggesting 'wire jtt-registration to storeLead' because it follows the naming pattern of register/register-program — without checking the route first. JTT was deprecated to 410 Gone in spring 2026; storeLead would have been re-added to dead code
antiPatterns.instead: Always Read or Grep the route file before suggesting pipeline integration. Look for: deprecation JSDoc, return status (200/410/etc.), redirect logic. Only suggest edits when route is actively serving traffic

antiPatterns.description: When committing a focused fix, sweeping up unrelated tracked-file modifications already present in the working tree
antiPatterns.avoid: git add -A or git add . when the tree has a focused leads-pipeline fix PLUS unrelated program data edits, ProgramCard tweaks, and auto-managed compound learning files
antiPatterns.instead: Stage only files in the plan's Files to Create/Modify list (or the focused commit's scope). Leave the rest dirty for the user/owner to commit separately. Be explicit in the response that unrelated dirty files were skipped, and what they look like — so the user can ship them as a coherent second commit

antiPatterns.description: Using 'echo "$SECRET" | npx vercel env add NAME production' to add a secret env var — echo appends a trailing newline that gets captured. If the secret is used in an HTTP header, Vercel build will fail with 'leading or trailing whitespace, not allowed in HTTP header values'
antiPatterns.avoid: echo "$SECRET" | npx vercel env add CRON_SECRET production
antiPatterns.instead: printf '%s' "$SECRET" | npx vercel env add CRON_SECRET production. printf does NOT add a trailing newline. After adding, sanity-check by curl-ing the cron endpoint with the local secret and confirming 200 (proves header parses cleanly)

antiPatterns.description: Leaving lead/contact records fragmented across Notion + CRM + Supabase + Zapier when external channels (Meta lead ads, manual imports) bypass the website code path. Compounds: future dashboards, attribution, retention reports all see partial data
antiPatterns.avoid: Adding new lead channels (Meta lead ads, GHL, etc.) without a path that funnels back to the primary record (Supabase leads). Audit found 165 of 251 leads bypassed Supabase, making it 66% incomplete
antiPatterns.instead: Audit storage targets quarterly. When a gap is found, add a daily cron mirror from the orphan source into the primary record, keyed on the orphan's natural ID for idempotency. See pattern notion-supabase-mirror-cron

antiPatterns.description: Tests that readFileSync(scriptSource) and assert via regex match
antiPatterns.avoid: expect(checkerSource).toMatch(/emailScanRoot\s*=\s*["']assets\/emails["']/) — breaks on reformatting, type annotations, Set member reordering, accidental flags
antiPatterns.instead: Export the rule function + constants from the script. Wrap main() in isMainModule guard. Write behavior tests that import and call the function with synthetic fixtures, assert on returned values

antiPatterns.description: try { ... } catch { return [] } in a code path that enforces legal compliance
antiPatterns.avoid: getTrackedEmailTemplates() returning [] silently on git failure — CAN-SPAM check then passes without ever running
antiPatterns.instead: console.warn loudly + fall back to a less-precise method (filesystem walk vs git ls-files). Bias toward false-positive over silent skip. Worst case: noise. Best case: catches violations

antiPatterns.description: Using line: 0 in a { line: number } Hit type to mean "file-level finding"
antiPatterns.avoid: reportData.push({ file, line: 0, value }) — printer renders foo.html:0 (impossible line number); type system cannot enforce the distinction
antiPatterns.instead: Type-narrow to { line: number | null } with null as the file-level marker. Printers render null as "(file)". Type system enforces at every consumer

antiPatterns.description: Defensive code that compiles but does nothing (cargo-cult escaping, redundant null checks, etc.)
antiPatterns.avoid: forbiddenHex.replace('#', '#') as 'defensive escaping' when # is not a regex metacharacter — pure no-op that misleads readers into thinking escaping is happening
antiPatterns.instead: If escaping is needed, write the actual escape: forbiddenHex.replace(/[.*+?^()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&"). If not needed, write the comment explaining why

antiPatterns.description: Adding a new file type (e.g. .html) to scanRoots without filtering domain-incompatible scanners
antiPatterns.avoid: Adding assets/emails to the React-side files[] loop where the font scanner flags Helvetica/Arial in legitimate email font-fallback stacks (48 false-positive ERRORS in one run)
antiPatterns.instead: Domain-aware routing at the dispatch layer. files[] (React-side scanners) excludes .html. HTML files go ONLY to the email scanner. Different domains = different rules = separate dispatch

antiPatterns.description: Tests that import a constant and assert its value, branded as "behavior tests"
antiPatterns.avoid: expect(emailScanRoot).toBe('assets/emails') and expect(emailForbiddenHexes.has('#d5d1ca')).toBe(true) — same anti-pattern as source-grep, just relocated to import-and-assert
antiPatterns.instead: Behavior tests should call the function and assert on output. If the constant matters, a behavior test that uses it implicitly verifies it (e.g. test #1 "flags forbidden hex #d5d1ca" fails if the hex is removed from the Set)
